summary: Context7 MCP config failed with 'url is not supported for stdio'.
root_cause: Context7 server block mixed STDIO fields with HTTP URL fields.
resolution: Use STDIO-only Context7 config with command='npx' and args=['-y','@upstash/context7-mcp']; do not mix url with command/args.
notes: User-level config outside repo was not edited because it may contain user secret material.

summary: Backend/frontend verification commands could not start because go and npm were not available in a previous default shell PATH.
root_cause: The earlier Codex shell environment reported 'command not found' for both go and npm.
resolution: Use the current Windows PowerShell workspace toolchain or activate the bundled/project toolchain before running checks; current workspace now resolves both go and npm.
notes: Resolved in the current workspace after toolchain availability was verified. No secrets or environment values were recorded.

summary: Go and npm checks may fail inside sandbox when they need home-directory build/module/cache/log paths.
root_cause: Managed sandbox blocks writes under the user home cache directories used by Go toolchain/module cache and npm cache/logs.
resolution: Retry verification commands with scoped escalation for `go test`, `go vet`, `npm ci` or localhost `curl` when the first failure is cache/write/bind related.
notes: Resolved after scoped sandbox escalation for verification commands. No secrets, env values, tokens or provider credentials were recorded.

summary: Cloudflare Dashboard-managed Mini App tunnel failed while VPN or local network filtering blocked Cloudflare edge connectivity; after VPN was disabled the connector registered, but the public hostname still returned 502 due to edge resets or stale connectors.
root_cause: Outbound cloudflared connectivity to Cloudflare edge on port 7844 was blocked or reset by VPN, firewall, antivirus, ISP routing or a stale cloudflared service/process. Multiple connectors for the same tunnel can leave Cloudflare routing to a broken local connector.
resolution: Disable VPN, stop all cloudflared processes/services, run exactly one dashboard-managed Mini App connector with protocol http2, verify local Vite/API first, then verify https://app.neiirohub.ru. If edge resets remain, test from another network or run the connector on another machine/server.
checks: Cloudflare remote ingress was app.neiirohub.ru -> http://127.0.0.1:5173
notes: Do not store or print tunnel tokens. Runtime logs/process args may have exposed a token before launcher cleanup; patched launcher avoids token command-line args and does not pass CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN to child cloudflared.

summary: Public /metrics probe returned Prometheus exposition from vk.neiirohub.ru while app.neiirohub.ru returned only HTML SPA fallback.
root_cause: A Cloudflare tunnel ingress route forwarded the public hostname to a local metrics-capable origin without a higher-priority /metrics deny rule. App-level private metrics guards can be bypassed when the tunnel/origin path hides public host or client headers from the Go process.
resolution: Add a higher-priority Cloudflare ingress/Public Hostname rule for the affected hostname and path ^/metrics(?:$|/) with service http_status:404, restart the active connector, keep Go /metrics guarded against public forwarded headers, and make the blackbox public metrics probe match Prometheus body content rather than plain HTTP 2xx.
checks: public HEAD showed vk.neiirohub.ru/metrics returned Prometheus text/plain
local app guard rejected Cloudflare-like forwarded headers with 404
blackbox public metrics probe now ignores HTML fallback and still detects real Prometheus exposition
notes: No metrics body, tunnel token, process command line, raw headers, secrets or user data were recorded.

summary: Provider output download failures could persist full remote output URLs into job errors, traces or DLQ records.
root_cause: artifactservice wrapped downloader failures with the original remote URL before returning the error to the worker failure path.
resolution: Redact URL-like substrings before recording or returning remote artifact download errors; keep only a generic download failure message and redaction marker.
checks: unit test asserts signed/private URL parts do not appear in SaveRemoteArtifact errors
notes: No provider URL, query string, prompt, artifact body or credential was recorded.

summary: When mock was present in the provider chain, the artifact downloader used a mock-aware HTTP fallback without SSRF protections.
root_cause: cmd/worker replaced the default artifactservice downloader with mock.NewDownloader, whose non-mock HTTP branch used the default HTTP client directly.
resolution: Make mock downloader handle only synthetic mock/data URLs and delegate real provider URLs to artifactservice's SSRF-hardened HTTP downloader.
checks: unit tests cover data URL handling, fallback delegation and rejection without fallback
notes: No target URL body, provider payload or credentials were recorded.

summary: Dev Postgres, Redis and MinIO ports were published on all interfaces with development credentials.
root_cause: Base docker-compose.yml used host port mappings without a 127.0.0.1 bind address.
resolution: Bind dev service ports to 127.0.0.1 so they remain reachable from local tools but are not exposed on LAN/VPN interfaces by default.
checks: docker compose config validates the resulting port bindings
notes: No database content, passwords or object data were recorded.

summary: Production configuration could still accept mock provider or mock payment settings and unknown provider-chain entries could be skipped later by worker startup.
root_cause: Config validation checked image/video/payment provider names but did not validate PROVIDER/PROVIDER_CHAIN or forbid mock providers in production.
resolution: Validate PROVIDER and each PROVIDER_CHAIN entry, and fail closed when production config contains mock generation or payment providers.
checks: unit tests cover unknown provider-chain entries and mock provider/payment rejection in production
notes: No environment values or secrets were recorded.

summary: A runtime-local Cloudflare tunnel config under .runtime was tracked even though .runtime is ignored.
root_cause: The file was added before or despite the ignore rule, so local tunnel metadata such as credentials-file paths remained in Git history/worktree diffs.
resolution: Remove the runtime config from Git tracking while keeping the local file ignored and generated by setup scripts.
checks: git status shows the file as a staged deletion rather than a modified tracked runtime config
notes: No credential file contents, tunnel token or Cloudflare secret was recorded.

summary: Go targeted tests failed in the managed sandbox when the default build cache under the user AppData directory was inaccessible.
root_cause: The test command tried to open compiled cache artifacts under a non-workspace GOCACHE path, and the sandbox denied access.
resolution: Run Go checks with GOCACHE set to a workspace-local path such as D:\vk_agregator\.cache\go-build.
checks: go test targeted packages passed with workspace-local GOCACHE
notes: No source code, secrets, prompts, launch params or environment values were recorded.

summary: Mini App Playwright smoke reported all tests passed but the npm command did not exit when Playwright owned the Vite webServer on Windows.
root_cause: The integrated Playwright webServer lifecycle could leave the command waiting after tests completed in the local Windows shell.
resolution: Run the npm smoke script through a small Node runner that starts Vite directly, runs Playwright with the config webServer disabled, and terminates the owned Vite process in finally.
checks: npm --prefix web/miniapp run e2e:smoke passed and exited with code 0
notes: No secrets, prompts, full launch params, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: Initial web/admin npm install timed out without creating package-lock.json or node_modules.
root_cause: The managed sandbox install path stalled before dependency resolution completed; rerunning with explicit no-audit/no-fund/prefer-offline outside the sandbox completed quickly.
resolution: Use npm --prefix web/admin install --no-audit --no-fund --prefer-offline --loglevel=warn when bootstrapping the admin package in this Windows workspace.
notes: No package tokens, environment values, prompts, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: web/admin typecheck failed because TypeScript could not resolve the side-effect CSS import in main.tsx.
root_cause: The new Vite app was missing the vite/client ambient type reference that declares CSS module side-effect imports.
resolution: Add web/admin/src/vite-env.d.ts with a vite/client reference.
notes: No secrets, auth headers, prompts, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: web/admin Vite production build failed during CSS post-processing because esbuild was not installed in the package.
root_cause: Vite 8 used esbuild for CSS post-processing, but the new admin package did not list esbuild as a devDependency.
resolution: Add esbuild to web/admin devDependencies and refresh package-lock.json.
notes: No secrets, auth headers, prompts, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: web/admin API client tests failed in Vitest because the client used window.setTimeout in a Node test environment.
root_cause: The safe API client depended on the browser-only window global for request timeout handling, while unit tests run outside a browser DOM by default.
resolution: Use globalThis.setTimeout and globalThis.clearTimeout for timeout handling so the client works in browser and Node test environments.
checks: npm --prefix web/admin run test passed after fix
notes: No secrets, auth headers, prompts, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: web/admin npm install failed repeatedly with npm Invalid Version while adding a devDependency.
root_cause: npm 11.13.0 on Node 24.16.0 failed inside Arborist dedupe while processing the local esbuild dependency tree; the failure happened before package-lock changes were written.
resolution: Add the Playwright devDependency and lockfile entries from the existing workspace lock data, and use the admin smoke runner fallback to an already-installed workspace Playwright CLI for this local machine until node_modules can be refreshed with a known-good npm path.
checks: npm --prefix web/admin run e2e:smoke passed after fallback runner/config was added
notes: Sanitized entry only; no tokens, prompts, raw payloads, private URLs or PII were recorded.

summary: Output moderation could allow generated text because the worker checked only the originating prompt and saved assistant dialog context before the output moderation gate.
root_cause: The provider success path called dialog context completion before output moderation and passed only the stored prompt to moderationservice.Input for the output stage.
resolution: Pass generated text to output moderation and persist assistant dialog context only after the output moderation decision allows delivery.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no prompt bodies, generated content, launch params, auth headers, raw provider payloads, private URLs, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Manual refund replay with the same operator idempotency key could return a provider_pending refund without retrying the provider operation, leaving a committed refund debit stranded after crash windows.
root_cause: RefundIntent treated any existing refund row as final idempotent output, even when the row status was created or provider_pending and provider state had not been durably recorded.
resolution: Existing created/provider_pending refunds now resume provider CreateRefund with the stable internal payrefund:<refund_id> idempotency key; refund.succeeded webhooks remain unprocessed with an unsupported signal until explicit reconciliation exists.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no provider payloads, payment credentials, auth headers, user PII, private URLs or secrets were recorded.

summary: Synchronous OpenAI/DeepInfra outputs could be lost on retry after provider submit succeeded but before poll/save completed.
root_cause: Provider adapters cached sync outputs in process memory and GenerationWorker.persistTask did not copy submitted Result/CompletedAt/ErrorClass into provider_tasks, so a restart could turn a paid success into task_not_found or duplicate submit reliance.
resolution: Sync OpenAI/DeepInfra Submit now returns terminal ProviderTask.Result and the worker persists submitted durable fields; retry resumes from provider_tasks.result without calling provider Poll.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no prompt bodies, generated content, provider payloads, auth headers, private URLs, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Payment webhook HTTPS and rate-limit decisions could trust caller-supplied forwarded headers without verifying the remote peer was a configured trusted proxy.
root_cause: cmd/provider-webhook accepted X-Forwarded-Proto, Forwarded and CF-Visitor directly, YOOKASSA_WEBHOOK_IP_ALLOWLIST_ENABLED had no enforced allowlist, and the shared rate limiter keyed on spoofable X-Forwarded-For.
resolution: Webhook ingress now trusts forwarded headers and forwarded client IP only from configured PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_TRUSTED_PROXIES, enforces configured YooKassa IP/CIDR allowlist when enabled, and rate limiting keys on the socket peer by default.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no request bodies, provider payloads, auth headers, source IP values from production, private URLs, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Billable Mini App generation/payment endpoints could accept missing or weak client idempotency keys, making user retries harder to bind to a stable client operation.
root_cause: The BFF generated or accepted insufficiently bounded idempotency input instead of requiring a bounded caller-supplied X-Idempotency-Key scoped to the verified VK user.
resolution: Require X-Idempotency-Key length 8..128 with a safe character set for Mini App job, chat and payment-intent creation; scope persisted keys by verified VK user.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no launch params, prompts, user identifiers, private URLs, auth headers, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Payment confirmation URLs needed an explicit HTTPS gate before persistence and user-visible rendering.
root_cause: Provider confirmation_url values were treated as openable links without a shared absolute-HTTPS validation step on both backend persistence and frontend navigation.
resolution: Reject unsafe provider confirmation URLs before saving provider state; render and open Mini App payment links only after absolute HTTPS normalization.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no payment payloads, auth headers, launch params, private URLs, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Oversized HTTP artifact downloads could be silently truncated at the remote byte cap and stored as corrupted media.
root_cause: The downloader read exactly maxRemoteBytes through io.LimitReader and had no maxRemoteBytes+1 sentinel check to distinguish an exact-limit body from an over-limit body.
resolution: Read maxRemoteBytes+1 and return an explicit too-large error when the sentinel byte is present; regression tests cover exact limit and limit+1 behavior.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no provider URLs, private artifact URLs, payload bytes, prompts, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Production real media providers could be configured without artifact scanning, and unsupported video scanning could be treated as allowed.
root_cause: ARTIFACT_SCANNER defaulted to none in the production example and config validation did not require a media artifact scanner for real providers; the OpenAI scanner returned nil for unsupported video media.
resolution: Production real provider config now requires ARTIFACT_SCANNER=openai, the production example enables it, and unsupported OpenAI scanner media types fail closed.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no provider payloads, artifact bytes, prompts, private URLs, auth headers, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Mini App job DTOs could expose output artifact IDs before the job reached terminal success, even though artifact byte access was separately guarded.
root_cause: newJobDTO copied job.OutputArtifactIDs for every status, including result_ready and rejected states, while the artifact download endpoint enforced succeeded plus allowed moderation.
resolution: Mini App DTOs now return output_artifact_ids only for succeeded jobs; result_ready/rejected/pending states expose an empty list.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no artifact storage keys, private URLs, prompts, launch params, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Non-positive PRICES overrides were rejected at billing runtime but not during config validation, delaying fail-closed feedback until job estimation/reservation paths.
root_cause: envPriceMap parsed zero/negative prices into Config.PriceOverrides and Config.Validate did not check them, while billingservice.Estimate and ReserveWith rejected them later.
resolution: Config.Validate now rejects empty operations and non-positive PRICES amounts at startup; billing runtime guards remain as defense in depth.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no env values beyond non-secret config keys, prompts, private URLs, auth headers, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: Production video jobs could fail permanently at artifact save because ARTIFACT_SCANNER=openai is required with real providers while the OpenAI scanner rejected MediaTypeVideo fail-closed.
root_cause: Config.Validate forced ARTIFACT_SCANNER=openai in production with real generation providers, but openai.Moderator.Scan returned an error for video bytes so saveOutputs never reached delivery or capture.
resolution: OpenAI artifact Scan now skips video, audio and document bytes; text and image remain scanned. Video safety stays in the worker pipeline via output text moderation, media probe and safe delivery variants. Supersedes the video fail-closed behavior introduced in err_20260613_media_output_scanner_fail_open_config for binary video artifacts only.
notes: Audit/debug session 5c156c via Bugbot subagent. Commit 6c8f8ef5 on fastlife_dev. No artifact bytes, prompts, provider payloads, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: refund.* webhook events stayed processed_at IS NULL forever and could head-of-line block payment.succeeded processing in the FIFO unprocessed inbox batch.
root_cause: WebhookProcessor.ProcessEvent returned ErrWebhookUnsupported for refund events without MarkEventProcessed; ListUnprocessedEvents orders by received_at ASC with a small batch limit.
resolution: Added ackInboxEvent to mark refund and empty-provider-payment-id inbox rows processed without ledger mutation; manual refund reconciliation remains operator-driven. Supersedes the intentional unprocessed refund backlog noted in err_20260613_payment_refund_pending_not_resumed for inbox-only ack only.
notes: Audit/debug session 5c156c via Security Review subagent. Commit 6c8f8ef5 on fastlife_dev. No payment payloads, provider credentials, user PII or secrets were recorded.

summary: Production YooKassa webhooks behind nginx or Cloudflare could be rejected with HTTP 426 when PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_TRUSTED_PROXIES was empty even though HTTPS was required.
root_cause: cmd/provider-webhook isSecureRequest ignores X-Forwarded-Proto unless the socket peer is in PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_TRUSTED_PROXIES; .env.prod.example shipped with an empty list and production config did not fail fast at startup.
resolution: Production Validate now requires PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_TRUSTED_PROXIES when PAYMENT_PROVIDER=yookassa; .env.prod.example documents trusted proxy CIDRs and enables YOOKASSA_WEBHOOK_IP_ALLOWLIST with a placeholder allowlist to fill from provider docs.
notes: Audit/debug session 5c156c. Commit 6c8f8ef5 on fastlife_dev. Operators must replace placeholder CIDRs with deployment-specific proxy and YooKassa source ranges. No live IP values, webhook bodies, auth headers or secrets were recorded.

summary: VPS production deploy failed during Dockerfile.miniapp npm ci because package-lock.json was missing optional dependency entries required by the current package.json dependency graph.
root_cause: Local checks used an existing node_modules install and did not exercise a clean npm ci; the committed web/miniapp/package-lock.json lacked transitive optional packages for lightningcss and rolldown bindings.
resolution: Regenerated web/miniapp/package-lock.json with npm install --package-lock-only, restored local node_modules, and reran Mini App build, lint and tests before redeploying.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no npm auth tokens, registry credentials, env secrets or deployment .env values were recorded.

summary: start-all.ps1 failed from Git Bash because Windows backslashes in the -File path were swallowed into scriptsdevstart-all.ps1.
root_cause: Git Bash treats backslash as an escape character, so scripts\dev\start-all.ps1 was passed to PowerShell as scriptsdevstart-all.ps1.
resolution: Use forward slashes from Git Bash: powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/dev/start-all.ps1 -NoWait. Use .\scripts\dev\start-all.ps1 only from PowerShell.
checks: Codex terminal snapshot showed PowerShell -File path error before script execution
notes: Sanitized entry only; no secrets, tunnel tokens, launch params or PII were recorded.

summary: start-all failed during docker compose up because docker-compose.observability.yml referenced external network vk-ai-aggregator-prod that did not exist locally.
root_cause: The observability compose overlay attaches exporters to an external prod-named network, but a fresh local Docker Desktop has no such network.
resolution: start-observability.ps1 now creates the configured COMPOSE_NETWORK_NAME or default vk-ai-aggregator-prod network before compose up.
checks: docker compose progressed past network creation and started containers after the network was created
notes: No container data, DB contents, credentials or secrets were recorded.

summary: Local migrate up failed with checksum mismatch for 000015_product_observability_indexes after branch history changed the migration file.
root_cause: The local Docker Postgres volume had already recorded the old checksum for a migration whose file contents later changed in the working tree.
resolution: For local dev only, run start-all.ps1 with -ResetDevData after explicit approval to recreate dev Docker volumes; never use this on production data.
checks: Fresh local Postgres later showed schema_migrations and app tables present
notes: Sanitized entry only; no row data, payment data, secrets or PII were recorded.

summary: API appeared to hang at Starting API but actually exited immediately because PRICES contained image_generate=0.
root_cause: .env had been corrected, but .env.ps1 overlay still set PRICES=text_generate=1,image_generate=0,video_generate=50, and config validation rejects non-positive price overrides at startup.
resolution: Set image_generate to a positive amount in .env.ps1 and align .env.example, README and docs/VIDEO_GENERATION.md with image_generate=1.
checks: api-live.log showed invalid configuration before fix
rg found no remaining image_generate=0 in env/docs files after fix
notes: No real env secrets or user data were recorded; only non-secret config key names and sanitized price values were included.

summary: cloudflared startup log included a raw CLOUDFLARED_TUNNEL_TOKEN value inherited from the parent process environment.
root_cause: start-all.ps1 imported .env into the process, set TUNNEL_TOKEN for cloudflared, but did not remove CLOUDFLARED_TUNNEL_TOKEN before Start-Process; cloudflared logs selected environment variable names and values at startup.
resolution: start-all.ps1 now removes CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARED_TUNNEL_TOKEN from the child process environment and passes only TUNNEL_TOKEN. The currently open runtime log is locked by the running connector; delete or redact .runtime/start-all/cloudflared-live.err after stopping the tunnel, and rotate the token if it may have been exposed beyond the local machine.
checks: cloudflared log showed routes registered and token exposure at startup
script patch removes CLOUDFLARED_TUNNEL_TOKEN before future Start-Process calls
notes: The token value itself was not copied into this entry or chat. Do not commit runtime logs or tunnel tokens.

summary: YooKassa could redirect a new top-up attempt to an old public app URL even after YOOKASSA_RETURN_URL_MINIAPP was corrected.
root_cause: paymentservice reused an active waiting intent by user/source/product only; it did not persist or compare the server-owned return_url used when the provider payment was created.
resolution: Persist resolved return_url in payment intent metadata for new intents and reuse an active waiting intent only when product and resolved return_url both match. Existing legacy waiting intents without return_url metadata are not reused when a non-empty return_url is requested.
notes: Sanitized entry only; no YooKassa payloads, confirmation URLs with tokens, user contacts or secrets were recorded.

summary: Mini App create menu and VK bot video menu did not show new video models after provider router integration.
root_cause: Local env still had FEATURE_VIDEO_ROUTER_ENABLED=false and old/disabled route flag names; PoYo routes also could not be enabled because POYO_BASE_URL was empty, and the Hailuo menu overlay used a misspelled HAIUO prefix.
resolution: Enabled local APIMart and Runway route flags, left PoYo routes disabled until POYO_BASE_URL is filled, corrected APIMART_BASE_URL to the APIMart host, and fixed the HAILUO spelling in .env.ps1.
checks: pending: service restart to reload local env
notes: Only non-secret flag names and route names were recorded. Do not enable PoYo routes until base URL is known.

summary: go test failed before package setup because the default Go build cache under the Windows user profile returned access denied.
root_cause: The test process could not write one compiled object in the default AppData go-build cache path.
resolution: Run local checks with GOCACHE pointed inside the repo runtime directory, for example PowerShell: $env:GOCACHE = (Join-Path (Get-Location) '.runtime\go-build-cache'); go test ./internal/service/paymentservice.
notes: No source code, env values, credentials or generated cache contents were recorded.

summary: Opening Mini App chat could repeatedly request /miniapp/chat/conversations/{id}/messages and receive 404, leaving the chat UI in a broken state.
root_cause: The frontend persisted an active chat id in localStorage, but on a fresh session the local chat list may only contain the default thread; the UI still requested history for the stale active id and ignored the 404 response instead of recovering.
resolution: Mini App chat now falls back when activeId is not present locally and treats 404 history loads as stale server conversation ids, removing non-draft stale chats instead of keeping a broken selection.
notes: No launch params, user ids, prompts, messages or PII were recorded.

summary: Mini App startup could feel slow because several backend requests independently waited for VK launch params resolution.
root_cause: The API client cached only the final launch params value, not the in-flight resolution promise, so concurrent startup requests could duplicate bridge wait work before the cache was populated.
resolution: Added in-flight launch params promise caching so concurrent API requests share one launch params resolution before hitting /miniapp/* endpoints.
notes: No launch params values, user ids or VK payloads were recorded.

summary: After host key reset, SSH login to the VPS failed for root, ubuntu, @admin, admin and codex even though the provider panel showed a successful VNC password change.
root_cause: The provider action changed the VNC or panel password for user @admin, not the Linux account password or SSH authorized_keys inside Ubuntu. The rebuilt VPS also lost the previous codex user/key.
resolution: Use the provider panel to add the codex public SSH key without replacing existing colleague keys, or perform an OS root password reset/rescue-mode password change. VNC password alone is insufficient for SSH automation.
checks: paramiko password-auth attempts returned AuthenticationException for plausible usernames
ssh old codex key returned Permission denied
notes: No passwords, private keys, host private material or server secrets were recorded.

summary: DEV Cloudflare hostnames returned 502 even though local reverse-proxy smoke passed for VK health, Mini App frontend, Mini App BFF and YooKassa webhook routes.
root_cause: cloudflared selected QUIC transport and repeatedly failed edge connections with timeout/no recent network activity, so Cloudflare saw a healthy or intermittently connected tunnel but public requests could not reliably stream to the local origin.
resolution: Configure the DEV tunnel launcher to pass cloudflared --protocol http2, with CLOUDFLARED_PROTOCOL=http2 in dev.env/.env.dev.example. This avoids UDP/QUIC path issues and uses TCP/443 transport.
checks: cloudflared-live.err contained repeated QUIC timeout/control stream failures
notes: No tunnel token, env secret, request body, launch params or private URL was recorded.

summary: Balanced video route video_kling_o3_standard failed immediately with provider invalid_request and released the reserved credits.
root_cause: The PoYo adapter still used the old submit/status envelope shape with top-level inputs/parameters and fields such as audio/reference_images, while current PoYo Kling O3 docs use POST /api/generate/submit with model plus input object and code/data task responses.
resolution: Update the PoYo adapter to send model plus input, use sound=false for Kling O3 no-audio, send image_urls for image inputs, parse code/data submit responses, parse code/data/files status responses, and keep provider payloads/private URLs out of logs/admin metadata.
checks: local worker metrics showed poyo/video_kling_o3_standard invalid_request and billing release success before the fix
notes: No prompt, API key, auth header, raw provider payload, private output URL or launch params were recorded.

summary: Hailuo/APIMart video jobs failed during provider submit before a provider_task was created.
root_cause: dev.env pointed APIMART_BASE_URL at the wrong API host, so the worker posted APIMart video generation requests to a non-APIMart-compatible endpoint.
resolution: Set APIMART_BASE_URL in dev.env to the APIMart v1 host and restart the local dev worker so the adapter reads the corrected base URL.
checks: local dev worker rebuilt and restarted
notes: No API key, prompt, provider payload, auth header or provider private URL was recorded.

summary: PoYo/Kling video submit succeeded but the job later failed through poll errors/rate limiting and released credits.
root_cause: External async video providers were polled immediately after submit; for providers that need task propagation time this can trigger rate-limit or transient provider errors before the task reaches a stable status.
resolution: For APIMart, PoYo and Runway video tasks, GenerationWorker now enqueues provider_poll instead of polling inline, and PollWorker applies the configured worker backoff before the first external video poll and between pending/processing polls.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded.

summary: Browser console reported SVG path parse error: path d expected number near the media download icon.
root_cause: The download icon path used an invalid compact command sequence without a numeric coordinate in MediaResultPreview.
resolution: Replace the malformed path with a valid download icon path while keeping the same SVG size and stroke styling.
notes: No user content, artifact URL or launch params were recorded.

summary: Browser warned that aria-hidden was blocked because a focused settings history button stayed inside a hidden tab panel.
root_cause: Tab panels used aria-hidden while focus could still remain on a descendant during tab switching. CSS display:none already hides inactive panels, so aria-hidden was redundant and conflicted with focus.
resolution: Blur the focused element before changing tabs and remove aria-hidden from app tab panels, leaving visibility controlled by the existing active class/CSS.
notes: No user content, PII or launch params were recorded.

summary: PoYo completed Kling O3 and Seedance tasks, but Mini App never received videos because local jobs had already moved to failed_terminal.
root_cause: Worker treated transient HTTP/decode/rate-limit errors while polling an already-submitted external async video task as provider job failure. After retry budget was exhausted, credits were released and the job became terminal even though the provider task later reached finished with files.
resolution: For APIMart, PoYo and Runway video tasks, retryable poll transport errors now keep the existing provider task active, leave the job in provider_pending/provider_processing, and requeue provider_poll with increasing backoff. Terminal failure is reserved for provider task failed/cancelled or exhausted high poll transport budget.
checks: safe PoYo status check showed finished/files=1 for existing task ids
local dev worker rebuilt and restarted
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider response, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs were not revived because their credit reservations had already been released.

summary: Hailuo 2.3 Fast failed with invalid_request even though the Mini App sent one reference image.
root_cause: Initial hypothesis was incomplete: fast_pretreatment is not the Hailuo Fast model selector and did not resolve the provider-side validation error.
resolution: Superseded by err_20260619_apimart_hailuo_fast_requires_upload_before_generation.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider response, reference image bytes, storage key, auth header or launch params were recorded. Existing failed job was not retried because its reservation had already been released.

summary: Hailuo 2.3 Fast kept returning invalid_request for Mini App reference-image jobs; no provider task id was stored because submit failed before acceptance.
root_cause: APIMart upload documentation states base64 image data is no longer supported directly in generation APIs. Worker passed a sanitized data:image URL as first_frame_image, but APIMart now requires POST /v1/uploads/images first and then using the returned URL in /v1/videos/generations.
resolution: APIMart adapter now uploads data URL first-frame images as multipart file to /uploads/images, validates MIME and 20MB limit, uses the returned HTTP URL for first_frame_image, keeps public HTTP image URLs direct, and leaves fast_pretreatment independent from the Fast model.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, reference image bytes, storage key, uploaded provider URL, auth header, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs stay failed because their reservations were already released.

summary: Successful async video jobs could feel much slower than the provider render because status polling reused the generic retry backoff and eventually waited up to 30 seconds between pending/processing checks.
root_cause: Provider task pending/processing requeue used the same exponential retry backoff as error retries. This is too conservative for normal async status polling and adds result-detection latency after a provider task is already complete.
resolution: Worker now has separate WORKER_PROVIDER_POLL_BASE_DELAY and WORKER_PROVIDER_POLL_MAX_DELAY settings for async provider status checks. Local dev defaults were set to 1s/5s while keeping error retry backoff unchanged.
checks: local dev worker and miniapp Docker rebuild
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, private URL, launch params or PII was recorded. This reduces our detection delay but does not shorten provider-side render time.

summary: Runway Gen-4 Turbo output could become 16:9 when the user provided a vertical reference image.
root_cause: Mini App video job params did not include a trusted aspect_ratio, so the video route resolver used the first allowed route aspect ratio, which is 16:9 for Runway and other multi-aspect routes.
resolution: Persist uploaded image dimensions in artifact metadata and derive the closest allowed server-side aspect ratio from owned reference artifacts before billing estimate, job creation and provider submit. Runway Gen-4 Turbo now supports official 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1 and 21:9 mappings. Client-provided aspect_ratio remains ignored.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, artifact storage key, private URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Future true video-reference uploads should derive aspect from media probe metadata using the same backend-owned rule.

summary: Runway Gen-4 Turbo jobs with newly allowed aspect ratios failed before provider submit with unsupported_capability.
root_cause: Route catalog allowed Runway Gen-4 Turbo aspect ratios 4:3, 3:4 and 21:9, but the worker default provider media contract still allowed only 16:9, 9:16 and 1:1. The worker rejected the job before creating a provider_task, so no Runway HTTP request was made.
resolution: Align the Runway Gen-4 Turbo worker media contract with the route catalog and official Runway ratio mapping. Add a cmd/worker test that asserts the default Runway contract includes 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1 and 21:9.
checks: local dev worker Docker image rebuilt and container healthy
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, artifact ids, private URLs, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs remain terminal because their reservations were already released.

summary: A PoYo async video task could finish on the provider side after the local VK bot job had already been marked failed_terminal with credits released.
root_cause: Async video provider poll transport errors were capped at 20 attempts while local dev polling used short 1s/5s delays. A flaky status endpoint could exhaust the transport-error budget before slow video generation completed, leaving the provider_task pending locally and never downloading the finished provider output.
resolution: Increase the async video poll transport-error budget to 180 attempts and keep transient poll failures in provider_pending instead of terminal/refund at the old threshold. VK failure notices now distinguish provider/generation failures from media delivery/processing failures so token/upload issues are not confused with poll failures.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing terminal jobs remain terminal because their reservations were already released.

summary: Nano Banana 2 image requests with unsupported aspect_ratio could reach PoYo submit instead of failing locally.
root_cause: PoYo image validation used effectiveImageSize/effectiveImageResolution, which silently defaulted unsupported client values to safe defaults before checking allowlists.
resolution: Validate non-empty image aspect_ratio, size and resolution against allowlists before applying defaults, so invalid values fail closed before HTTP submit.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, private URL, launch params or PII was recorded.

summary: Nano Banana 2 image jobs could fail with invalid_request before a provider_task was persisted, while GPT Image 2 succeeded.
root_cause: The PoYo adapter sent Nano Banana 2 requests with an implicit 2K resolution while server-side billing reserved the 1K-priced 5 provider credits route. OpenAPI documents 1K as the schema default and prices 2K/4K higher, so the default request shape and estimate were not aligned.
resolution: Default Nano Banana 2 provider submits to 1K, estimate Nano Banana 2 provider credits by requested resolution, and keep 2K/4K only for future explicit backend-owned quality selection. The VK photo model menu now also exposes Nano Banana Pro through the existing feature-gated command.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs remain terminal with credits released.

summary: APIMart image jobs from VK could use a less deterministic request shape than the public APIMart image generation examples.
root_cause: VK photo job params did not pin an explicit backend-owned image size, so the worker could reuse the global image size default. APIMart image submit also serialized official_fallback:false even though the field is optional and disabled.
resolution: Persist size=1:1 with VK bot image jobs after backend-owned quality selection, carry selected resolution/image_quality to the worker, and omit official_fallback unless it is explicitly enabled.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Provider-side content policy can still reject unsafe prompts; this fix only normalizes backend-owned request shape.

summary: Provider image policy failures could be shown as temporary rate-limit/overload errors instead of content rejection.
root_cause: APIMart and PoYo error classifiers checked the substring rate before moderation terms. Provider policy messages containing words like generated matched rate inside the word and were normalized as rate_limited.
resolution: Classify explicit moderation/policy/safety/prohibited/filtered/violation terms before rate-limit terms, and add mocked provider tests for APIMart empty filtered image output and PoYo platform-regulation messages.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Provider-side policy can still reject unsafe prompts; credits remain released on technical/provider rejection failures.

summary: DeepInfra image models were not exposed under their real model names, and Mini App jobs could pass quality tokens as DeepInfra image size.
root_cause: The public image catalog still treated legacy aliases as user-facing choices, while DeepInfra native image endpoints expect pixel sizes such as 1024x1024 instead of 1K/2K/4K quality tokens.
resolution: Expose DeepInfra image models as ByteDance Seedream 4.5 and Stability AI SDXL Turbo, keep legacy aliases accepted but hidden, and normalize DeepInfra image size to pixel dimensions at the worker/provider boundary.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Frontend still submits public model IDs only; provider model codes remain server-side.

summary: PoYo Nano Banana 2 could generate successfully on the provider side while the local job failed immediately with provider_internal_error and released credits.
root_cause: The PoYo adapter accepted task_id but not the provider response shape that uses id/uuid/job_id fields. Submit could create a provider task, but the adapter treated the accepted response as empty submit task id.
resolution: Parse task ids from data.id, data.uuid, data.job_id, task.*, and top-level id/uuid/job_id fields; also parse image output URLs from data.result/data.output shapes. Add mocked submit/poll regression tests using the observed provider id shape.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs remain terminal unless manually reconciled.

summary: An APIMart GPT Image 2 provider_task could be succeeded with an output artifact saved while the job stayed provider_pending and no delivery task remained queued.
root_cause: If the worker stopped after persisting the terminal provider_task and saving output artifacts but before final job status/delivery enqueue, PollWorker later ignored terminal provider_task rows instead of replaying the durable provider result.
resolution: PollWorker now applies durable terminal provider_task results, and saveOutputs skips re-downloading when the job already has enough output_artifact_ids. Add worker regression test for terminal provider_task recovery with an existing output artifact.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing orphaned jobs need a safe provider_poll requeue after deploying this fix.

summary: PoYo Nano Banana 2 could start and finish on the provider side while the bot immediately reported temporary generation failure and released credits.
root_cause: GenerationWorker deferred the first external poll only for video jobs. Nano Banana 2 is also an async provider task, so the worker polled PoYo image status immediately after submit and treated early status propagation errors, including task_not_found/transport classes, as terminal job failure.
resolution: Treat APIMart/PoYo/Runway image and video tasks as async media tasks: enqueue provider_poll after submit, use provider poll backoff before first poll, and keep bounded transient poll failures active for an accepted provider_task instead of releasing credits.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing failed jobs remain terminal because credits were already released.

summary: Nano Banana 2 provider task could finish on PoYo while local job stayed provider_pending and the bot kept showing generation in progress.
root_cause: PoYo status response returned progress as a decimal number such as 0.0, while the adapter decoded progress into an int. JSON decode failed before status/files parsing, so worker kept requeueing poll errors and never reached artifact save or delivery.
resolution: Decode PoYo progress as a flexible optional value accepting integer, decimal and string percentages. Add a mocked Nano Banana 2 image status test with decimal progress and image output URLs. Requeue the already accepted local provider task once after deploying the parser fix, without new provider submit.
notes: No prompt, API key, raw provider payload, provider task id, private output URL, launch params or PII was recorded. The affected local job recovered to succeeded, stored one output artifact, captured the reserved credits and sent photo delivery.

summary: VK bot top-up/profile screens did not consistently show the current credit balance, and a payment open-link button could fail in the VK client without a visible fallback URL.
root_cause: The detailed account and top-up flows bypassed the simple balance text path, payment messages only exposed the provider confirmation URL through a VK open_link keyboard button, and active pending bot intents could be reused even when their stored return_url no longer matched the current bot dialog return target.
resolution: Load server-side balance for profile, catalog, pending and payment-link screens; include the provider confirmation URL in the user-facing payment message text as a fallback; remove extra payload from the payment open_link button; create/reuse active bot intents only when the stored return_url matches the current vk.com/write-<group_id> target.
notes: No API keys, YooKassa secret, VK token, raw provider payload, payment confirmation URL, launch params or PII was recorded. Existing old payment messages can still contain stale keyboard data; users should create a fresh top-up payment after deployment.
